How to Play Starburst Slot in 2026 — The Complete Beginner’s Guide for South African Players

If you’ve ever browsed an online casino, scrolled past a slots ad, or heard a mate mention Starburst over braai — chances are it’s already on your radar. After more than a decade on the market, this game hasn’t just survived; it’s still one of the first slots operators push to new players.

So how do you actually play it? This guide breaks down how to play Starburst slot in plain, simple terms. No jargon, no padding. By the time you reach the FAQ, you’ll know every button, every feature, and every trap worth avoiding. Whether you’re spinning from Johannesburg, Cape Town, Durban, or anywhere else in the country — we’ve got you sorted.

Why Learn How to Play Starburst?

It’s genuinely beginner-friendly. No cascading reels, no multi-stage bonus rounds, no mechanics that take twenty minutes to grasp. There’s one key feature — the expanding wild — and everything else flows from it naturally.

It stretches your budget. Low volatility means smaller wins land fairly often rather than making you sit through long dry spells. Your rands last longer per session.

It plays well on mobile. Most South Africans play on their phones, and Starburst was built exactly for that. Fast loading, clean scaling to any screen size, no app download required.

The RTP is above average. At 96.09%, it returns more to players over time than many competing slots. Not a guarantee — but a solid foundation to start from.

Starburst Game Screen Explained

Before you spin a single reel, spend sixty seconds getting familiar with the layout.

The Reels (centre of the screen) The main playing grid — five reels, three rows, a standard 5×3 setup. Each reel spins independently and displays gem symbols, lucky sevens, gold bars, and the Starburst Wild.

The Paytable Button (top corner) Opens the full symbol pay chart. Shows exactly how many coins each symbol pays for three, four, or five of a kind. Worth a quick glance before your first spin.

Coin Value Selector (bottom left area) Controls the value of each coin you wager. Lower coin value, smaller overall bet per spin.

Bet Level Selector (next to Coin Value) Multiplies the number of coins wagered per payline. Levels run from 1 to 10.

Total Bet Display (bottom centre) Your actual cost per spin in ZAR. Always check this number before spinning.

Spin Button (large circular button, bottom right) One click, reels go.

Max Bet Button Instantly jumps the bet level to maximum. Use deliberately — not impulsively.

Autoplay Button Set between 10 and 1,000 automatic spins at a fixed bet. The game runs them back-to-back without you clicking each time.

Win Display Shows your payout after each spin. During a wild-triggered re-spin, it updates in real time.

Step-by-Step Guide: How to Play Starburst Slot

Step 1: Find a trusted online casino Head to a reputable platform that carries Starburst and accepts ZAR payments.

Step 2: Create an account or log in New player — fill out the registration form. Returning — just log in. Make sure your account is verified and your payment method is linked before you start.

Step 3: Navigate to Starburst Use the casino’s search bar, type “Starburst,” and open the game. Most modern platforms run it directly in your browser via HTML5. No download required.

Step 4: Try the demo first If the casino offers a free demo mode — use it. You play on virtual credits with zero financial risk. The best way to watch the expanding wilds fire a dozen times before you commit real rands.

Step 5: Set your coin value Start low. R0.20 per coin is sensible for a first session.

Step 6: Set your bet level Level 1 or 2 is fine for beginners. Your total bet per spin updates automatically.

Step 7: Check your total bet Before hitting spin, confirm the Total Bet figure is an amount you’re comfortable losing on a single spin.

Step 8: Hit the spin button Click the large circular arrow. The five reels spin and stop within roughly two seconds. Winning combinations light up immediately.

Step 9: Watch for the Starburst Wild If a rainbow-coloured star lands on reels 2, 3, or 4, it expands to fill the entire reel, triggers a free re-spin, and locks in place while the other reels spin again.

Step 10: Collect your winnings or keep going Had a good win? You can stop and cash out whenever you want. No pressure, no obligation.

How to Set Your Bet and Manage Your Bankroll

Understanding the Betting System

Starburst uses a two-part system — coin value and bet level — that together set your total stake per spin. The minimum bet is R1.80 per spin, the maximum R1,800.

Coin ValueBet LevelTotal Bet Per Spin
R0.101R1.80
R0.105R9.00
R0.501R9.00
R1.005R45.00
R2.0010R180.00
R10.0010R1,800.00

Most South African players land somewhere in the R5.00–R20.00 range for casual sessions — enough to feel the game without burning through a budget in ten minutes.

Practical Bankroll Tips

Work out your session budget before you open the game. Say you’ve set R200 aside. At R2.00 per spin, that’s 100 spins — a proper session. Decide on that number before you start.

Never raise your bet to chase a loss. Starburst runs on a Random Number Generator — every spin is completely independent. What just happened has zero bearing on what’s coming next.

Lower your stake if you want to play longer. It sounds obvious, but it’s easily forgotten once the wilds start firing.

Set a win limit too. Decide in advance what a “good result” looks like. If you’re up 50% on your starting balance, cashing out is almost always the smarter move.

Understanding the Core Features While Playing

Starburst Expanding Wilds Explained

The Starburst Wild is the rainbow-coloured, jewel-studded star. You cannot miss it — the screen lights up when it lands.

Here’s exactly what happens:

  • It only appears on reels 2, 3, and 4 — never on the first or last reel
  • The moment it lands, it expands vertically to fill the entire reel
  • It locks in place and substitutes for every other symbol on the grid
  • It immediately triggers a free re-spin
  • That wild stays locked while the other four reels spin again

Why only the middle three reels? Because Starburst pays both ways. A wild anchored in the centre of the grid creates winning paths extending left and right simultaneously.

Win Both Ways — What It Actually Means

Most slots pay only for combinations running from the leftmost reel to the right. Starburst does something different.

In practice:

  • A matching combination of three or more symbols counts as a win in either direction
  • All 10 paylines are scanned both ways on every spin
  • Near-misses that would be dead elsewhere often still pay here

If three orange gems land on reels 5, 4, and 3 reading right to left — that’s a win. On most other slots, it’s nothing.

Re-Spins — How the Chain Works

Re-spins are the closest Starburst has to a bonus round. Triggered entirely by the expanding wild.

The chain:

  1. A wild lands on reel 2, 3, or 4 → expands and locks → one free re-spin
  2. During that re-spin, a new wild lands on a different middle reel → also expands and locks → another free re-spin
  3. This chains up to a maximum of three consecutive re-spins

When two or three wilds are locked across the middle reels simultaneously, a huge portion of the grid is covered. Combined with Win Both Ways, that’s where the slot’s biggest base-game wins come from.

Starburst Demo vs Real Money Mode

What stays identical in demo mode:

  • The RTP of 96.09%
  • The frequency of expanding wilds
  • The re-spin chain mechanic
  • The visuals, sounds, and pace of the game
  • The Win Both Ways payline structure

What changes:

  • You play with virtual credits — no real money involved
  • You cannot withdraw anything from demo play

Use demo mode to watch the expanding wild trigger fifteen or twenty times before committing rands. It’s also a useful test: if 100 demo spins feel slow and you’re bored, high-volatility games are probably a better fit.

Common Beginner Mistakes and How to Avoid Them

Hitting Max Bet without thinking Jumping to R1,800 per spin before you’ve found your rhythm is a fast way to drain your balance. Start low, move up deliberately.

Confusing re-spins with free spins Starburst has no traditional free spins bonus round. No scatter symbol, no separate bonus screen. The re-spins are triggered by the expanding wild and capped at three.

Chasing losses with bigger bets Each spin is random and independent. Increasing your bet after a losing run doesn’t shift the odds — it just makes each subsequent loss hurt more.

Skipping the paytable The gap between the gold bar and the blue gem is significant. Not knowing that makes it hard to understand why some winning spins pay so much more than others.

Underestimating the pace of the game A spin resolves in under two seconds. Set a phone timer or autoplay loss limits before you start.

Treating the RTP as a per-session guarantee The 96.09% figure is a long-run average across millions of spins. In any single session, your actual return can swing considerably higher or lower.

Pro Tips for South African Players

Play in ZAR where possible. It avoids conversion fees and makes it far easier to track what you’re actually spending.

Starburst is light on data. It’s an HTML5 game with a small file size, so it loads fast even on mobile data.

Bonus terms first, always. Free spins on Starburst as part of a welcome offer sounds great — but some casinos exclude NetEnt classic slots from wagering contribution. Read the terms before you accept anything.

Treat it as entertainment. Think of each session like a movie ticket — money spent on an experience, not an investment.

Use the spacebar on desktop. It spins the reels just as effectively as clicking the button.

Test the demo before your first deposit at a new casino. If it loads cleanly and runs without lag, the real-money version will too.

Symbol Payout Reference Table

Quick reference for Starburst’s symbol payouts at Bet Level 1:

Symbol3 of a Kind4 of a Kind5 of a Kind
Gold Bar50 coins200 coins250 coins
Red Lucky 725 coins60 coins120 coins
Yellow Gem10 coins25 coins60 coins
Green Gem8 coins20 coins50 coins
Orange Gem7 coins15 coins40 coins
Blue Gem5 coins10 coins25 coins
Purple Gem5 coins10 coins25 coins
Starburst WildExpands on reels 2, 3, or 4 — substitutes all, triggers re-spin

At Bet Level 10, every coin payout is multiplied by 10.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I play Starburst slot for the first time? Log in to a casino carrying Starburst, open the game, set your coin value and bet level, and click the spin button. Wins trigger automatically when three or more matching symbols land on a payline in either direction. Look out for the Starburst Wild on reels 2, 3, or 4 — it expands to cover the full reel and gives you a free re-spin.

What Starburst controls do I actually need to know? The coin value selector, the bet level selector, the spin button, and autoplay. The Total Bet display shows your per-spin cost before you commit. Min bet is R1.80, max is R1,800.

How do I use Starburst demo mode? Find Starburst at a casino offering free play, then click “Demo” or “Try for Free.” The game loads with virtual credits. Everything behaves exactly as in the paid version — same wilds, same re-spins, same paylines — but with no financial risk.

What does the Starburst spin button do exactly? It’s the large circular arrow in the bottom right of the screen. One click sets all five reels spinning; they stop within about two seconds and any winning combinations pay out immediately. On desktop, the spacebar works as a shortcut.

How do Starburst expanding wilds work? The rainbow-coloured wild lands only on reels 2, 3, or 4. It immediately expands to cover the entire reel, locks in place, substitutes for all other symbols, and triggers a free re-spin. If another wild lands during that re-spin, the chain continues — up to three consecutive re-spins in total.

What does Win Both Ways actually mean? Winning combinations pay out whether they run left to right or right to left across the paylines. All 10 paylines are active in both directions on every spin.

How do I adjust the Starburst bet settings? Use Coin Value to set the value per coin and Bet Level to set how many coins you wager per payline (1–10). Your total bet per spin updates automatically. Minimum is R1.80 per spin, maximum R1,800.

Can I play Starburst on mobile in South Africa? Yes. It’s HTML5, runs in any modern Android or iOS browser, no app needed. The controls are thumb-accessible in portrait mode.

Does Starburst have free spins? Not in the traditional sense. There’s no scatter symbol, no separate bonus screen. What Starburst has is the re-spin loop — up to three consecutive free re-spins triggered by the expanding wild.

What is the RTP of Starburst and what does it mean for me? The RTP is 96.09%. For every R100 wagered across millions of spins, the game returns R96.09 on average. In a single session, your actual result can land well above or below that figure — it’s a long-run average, not a per-session promise.

Gambling involves risk. Play responsibly. If you feel your gambling is becoming problematic, contact the National Responsible Gambling Programme at 0800 006 008 (free, 24/7).
